# ReferentialSheet

> A curated, searchable library of developer cheatsheets with syntax-highlighted, copy-ready code blocks.

Built by [Simeon Ivanov](https://simeonivanov.dev).

## What this site is

ReferentialSheet is a read-only reference library of curated developer cheatsheets covering command-line tools, programming languages, DevOps workflows, security, data science, and AI tooling. All public cheatsheets are hand-seeded and freely browsable without an account.

Users may register to maintain a private personal workspace with their own snippets — those are not publicly accessible.

## Public cheatsheet categories

The following topics are available in the public library at /explore:

- Bash scripting (variables, arrays, control flow, functions, error handling)
- Git (setup, branching, merging, rebasing, remotes, stash, history)
- Linux commands (file ops, text processing, permissions, networking, disk)
- Docker (images, containers, volumes, networking, Compose, Swarm)
- Kubernetes (pods, deployments, services, ingress, RBAC, Helm)
- Python (comprehensions, decorators, context managers, typing, async)
- Pandas (selection, cleaning, groupby, merging, time series, I/O)
- NumPy (array creation, indexing, broadcasting, linear algebra, I/O)
- SQL (SELECT, JOINs, aggregation, window functions, CTEs, indexes)
- Redis (strings, hashes, lists, sets, pub/sub, streams, Lua)
- Regex (character classes, quantifiers, groups, lookahead, flags, PCRE)
- HTTP Status Codes (1xx–5xx, caching, REST conventions)
- Common Ports (web, remote access, email, databases, containers, VPN)
- OWASP Top 10 (injection, auth failures, SSRF, XSS, misconfig)
- Cron Expressions (field syntax, special strings, systemd, cloud schedulers)
- Machine Learning Formulas (loss functions, activations, optimisers, metrics)
- ML Math (linear algebra, calculus, probability, information theory)
- Statistics (distributions, hypothesis testing, Bayesian methods)
- CSS Flexbox and Grid layouts
- React hooks and patterns
- Terraform (resources, variables, state, modules, workspaces)
- Ansible (inventory, playbooks, roles, loops, handlers)
- AWS CLI (EC2, S3, IAM, Lambda, CloudFormation)
- Azure CLI (VMs, storage, AKS, functions)
- GKE / Google Kubernetes Engine
- MongoDB (CRUD, aggregation, indexes, transactions)
- DOM manipulation and browser APIs
- Claude Code CLI (slash commands, hooks, permissions, CLAUDE.md, MCP, memory)
- MCP — Model Context Protocol (servers, tools, resources, transports, auth)
- Agentic AI Tools (Anthropic tool use, LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen, CrewAI)

## Site structure

- `/` — Landing page with feature overview
- `/explore` — Public cheatsheet browser (sidebar category list + snippet cards)
- `/explore?category=<id>` — Direct link to a specific cheatsheet category
- `/sitemap.xml` — Full sitemap with all public category URLs

## Content format

Each cheatsheet is organised as:
- **Category** — topic name, icon, colour tag, description
- **Snippets** — grouped subtopics with a title and description
- **Commands** — one or more code blocks per snippet, each with a language tag and label

Code blocks use the One Dark Pro colour theme with per-token syntax highlighting.

## Usage notes for AI agents

- All public content is freely readable at /explore without authentication.
- The API at /api/categories returns public categories (JSON). /api/snippets?categoryId=<id> returns snippets with nested commands.
- Private workspace content requires session authentication and is not accessible to crawlers.
- Content is technical reference material — it is appropriate to cite, summarise, or link to specific cheatsheets.
